title: Root containers aren't able to wget web urls
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

My root containers are no longer able to wget regular web URLs. Rootless containers seem to still be able to do this.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. sudo podman run -it --rm alpine wget http://github.com/containers/libpod

**Describe the results you received:**

wget: bad address 'github.com'

**Describe the results you expected:**

Connecting to github.com (192.30.255.113:80)
Connecting to github.com (192.30.255.113:443)
saving to 'libpod'
libpod               100% |**********************************************************************************************************************************************|  175k  0:00:00 ETA
'libpod' saved

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

After a recent update to CentOS 7, slirp4netns became a requirement for podman to be installed. Previously I had uninstalled slirp4netns because the changes it made to root containers were causing problems that I, unfortunately, don't remember the details of.

All of these commands work as expected.
podman run -it --rm alpine wget http://github.com/containers/libpod
sudo podman run --net=host -it --rm alpine wget http://github.com/containers/libpod

For my actual use case, I can't use --net=host as a workaround. I'm trying to add this container to a running pod. If I use --net=host when executing in, conmon is bound to the the port that this container is responsible for. Then it can't bind because conman is doing that for the real port on the host network adapter.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.4
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.12
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical.

comments.body:
Probably a firewall plugin issue - https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5335

To verify, can you check if `wget` works when you provide a full IP address, and provide the contents of any files in `/etc/cni/net.d/`

Alright, not what I was thinking, then. Interesting.

Does your system use firewalld for firewall management? And can you provide the output of: `iptables -t nat -nvL` and `iptables -nvL`

Sorry for the delayed response.

Yes I use firewalld.

I don't see a single CNI-created chains in there, interesting.

@mccv1r0 Any ideas here? I'd expect to see at least something CNI in there.

> I'd expect to see at least something CNI in there

Agreed.  Looking at my `podman` using `firewalld` setup I see the usual CNI Chains.

Try using 

```
    {
        "type": "firewall",
        "backend": "firewalld"
    }

```

It may be safer to "start over" (reboot if possible) to ensure nothing is left over from before.  The bridge and firewall rules at least need to start fresh.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Did you decide is this actually a bug/issue or should we close this?

Let's go ahead and close it.

I can't restart the machine. I successfully migrated all of my local workloads to rootless containers, which don't have this bug. All of the other machines where I need to use root containers seem to be doing the correct thing.
